wonder: Does reality render when we are not looking at it?
activity_summary: Learner discussed whether an unseen room continues to exist or update when no one is observing it. The conversation used a game-engine metaphor to explore observation, continuity, and hidden state.
learner_explanation: If you are not looking at reality, maybe it is like a game that only renders what you see. But when you go back, things still seem like they were there.

parent_review.review_note: Accepted as evidence of abstract reasoning using a computational metaphor to explore observation and continuity.

subject_mappings.subject: philosophy
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner considered whether unobserved reality persists.

subject_mappings.subject: science
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner discussed observation and continuity in the physical world.

subject_mappings.subject: technology
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner used rendering as a computational metaphor.

evidence_items.description: Learner asked whether reality renders when not observed.

next_questions: What would be different if unseen rooms did not keep running?
How could we test whether something changed while we were away?
What is the difference between seeing something and knowing something?
non_capture_note: This receipt records one learning event and should not be used as a fixed identity label.
